context: The Binaca Geetmala countdown on Radio Ceylon was the definitive arbiter of Hindi film song popularity. 1955 was dominated by Raj Kapoor's landmark film Shree 420, whose 'Mera Joota Hai Japani' became an anthem of post-independence India. The snake-charmer film Nagin contributed two top-10 hits, while patriotic songs from Jagriti reflected the idealism of the Nehruvian era.

tracks.title: Meraa Dil Ye Pukaare Aa Jaa
De Di Hame Aazadi Bina Khadag Bina Dhaal
Chalo Chale Mann Sapano Ke Ganv Me
